title: Concomitant Predictors and Distal Outcomes
text: mixLCA enforces temporal separation three blocks variables: Antecedent concomitant predictors: variables come class formation shift probability belonging latent class (e.g., demographics). Contemporaneous manifest indicators: variables define classes (measurement model). Subsequent distal outcomes: variables consequences class membership contaminate . Mixing blocks naively produces double-counted information. Letting distal outcome help define classes mechanically inflates predictive power; letting concomitant predictor enter measurement model leaks variance class meaning. mixLCA keeps three blocks separate: concomitants enter multinomial logistic regression class membership, distals estimated measurement model converged, Bolck-Croon-Hagenaars (BCH) inverse-classification-error weighting. vignette walks pieces embedded health_screening dataset: synthetic 800-subject screening study four continuous biomarkers, age covariate, binary outcome.

title: Concomitant Predictors and Distal Outcomes
text: screening outcome consequence marker class. put measurement model; estimate separately BCH weighting. BCH method (Bolck, Croon, & Hagenaars, 2004; refined WLS Vermunt, 2010, Bakk, Tekle, & Vermunt, 2013) constructs class-specific inverse-classification-error weights fitted posteriors, fits class-specific regression distal outcome using weights. measurement model frozen distal step, gradient distal outcome can contaminate class meaning. Reading output: one class negative intercept (low baseline P(outcome = “yes”)), less-negative positive intercept (higher baseline risk). age slope within class small positive: class membership absorbs age signal, modest within-class residual age effect. classification error matrix bottom tells confident modal assignment . Diagonal dominance close 1 means BCH adjustment mild; values closer 0.5 mean classification uncertain BCH weights pulling strongly.

title: Introduction to mixLCA
text: mixLCA fits finite mixture models latent class analysis partitioned architecture: concomitant predictors enter multinomial logistic regression class membership; manifest indicators (continuous, categorical, mixed) define measurement model; distal outcomes estimated measurement model converges, Bolck-Croon-Hagenaars inverse-classification-error weighting. partition prevents distal outcomes leaking class meaning concomitants contaminating measurement structure. package supports two responses local dependence among manifest indicators: Direct effects categorical items (Hagenaars-Vermunt specification search via auto_bvr()). Spectral Local Dependence (SLD) categorical items: low-rank decomposition conditional Burt matrix (auto_sld()). continuous indicators, covariance structure can fully unrestricted, diagonal, sparsified via graphical lasso (dependence = "penalized").

title: Introduction to mixLCA
code: library(mixLCA) data(health_screening)  fit <- fit_lca(health_screening,                continuous  = c("marker_1", "marker_2", "marker_3", "marker_4"),                concomitant = ~ age,                n_classes   = 2,                control     = lca_control(n_starts = 10))  summary(fit, data = health_screening) plot(fit, type = "profiles")  distal(fit, health_screening, outcome ~ age, family = "binomial")

title: Introduction to mixLCA
text: Core fitting: fit_lca(), lca_control(): entry point optimizer settings. auto_bvr(): BVR-guided specification search. auto_sld(): adaptive Spectral Local Dependence rank selection. enumerate_lca(): fit range KK values. Diagnostics: fit_indices(): AIC, BIC, aBIC, entropy, ICL. bvr_tests(), bvr_categorical(): bivariate residual diagnostics. class_table(): modal-class x posterior cross-tabulation. Inference: predict() (S3 method): posteriors, modal class, full diagnostic frame. distal(): BCH-weighted regression distal outcomes. concomitant_se(), continuous_se(): asymptotic standard errors. get_posteriors(), get_loadings(), coef(): accessors. Visualization: plot() (S3 method) type = c("profiles", "bvr", "distal",   "uncertainty", "convergence", "categorical", "spectral_scree",   "spectral_loadings").

title: Spectral Local Dependence: Theory and Worked Example
text: local-independence assumption underwrites every classical LCA: given class kk, manifest indicators Y1,…,YJY_1, \ldots, Y_J statistically independent. practice assumption frequently fails. Two established responses exist. Direct effects (Hagenaars 1988; Vermunt 1999) add conditional probability table P(Yj∣Yj′,C=k)P(Y_j \mid Y_{j'}, C = k) one item pairs (j,j′)(j, j'). remedy targeted cheap local dependence sits small number identifiable pairs, scales O(J2)O(J^2) candidate pairs search produces dense parameter tables pair admitted. Latent trait extensions (Magidson Vermunt’s mixed mixture models, Bartolucci’s hidden-Markov LCA, factor-mixture models) add within-class continuous latent variable. handle distributed dependence break discrete-class story introduce numerical integration. Spectral Local Dependence (SLD) sits two. introduces rank-dkd_klow-dimensional shift class-conditional log response surface. shift sufficient capture broad correlated residuals, leaves class labels discrete M-step closed-form (eigendecomposition). vignette gives math, identification argument, implementation mixLCA, worked example.

text: EM one twist: E-step. Compute posteriors γik∝πk⋅softmaxJ(𝛈i(k))\gamma_{ik} \propto \pi_k \cdot \mathrm{softmax}_J(\boldsymbol{\eta}^{(k)}_i), usual. Form weighted conditional residual covariance 𝚺̂(k)=1∑iγik∑iγik𝐑i(k)𝐑i(k)⊤, \widehat{\boldsymbol{\Sigma}}^{(k)} =   \frac{1}{\sum_i \gamma_{ik}}   \sum_i \gamma_{ik} \, \mathbf{R}^{(k)}_i \mathbf{R}^{(k)\top}_i,  𝐑i(k)=𝐙i−𝛑(k)\mathbf{R}^{(k)}_i = \mathbf{Z}_i - \boldsymbol{\pi}^{(k)}. title: Spectral Local Dependence: Theory and Worked Example
text: Three strategies. Fixed rank. Pass spectral_rank = 2L (scalar, rank classes) spectral_rank = c(1L, 2L, 0L) (length-KK vector). Pooled basis. Set spectral_pool = TRUE estimate single spectral basis class-weighted average conditional Burt matrices, applied uniformly every class. sacrifices class- specific dependence structure sharper estimate dominant directions, useful sample size per class small. Adaptive search. auto_sld() runs greedy forward search: step identifies class largest unmodelled eigenvalue, tries adding one rank , accepts BIC (AIC, aBIC, ICL) improves. search stops class can improve rank cap reached. result class-specific ranks matched data’s actual residual structure.

Without spectral shift absorb information already encoded 𝛑(k)\boldsymbol{\pi}^{(k)} (within-item probabilities), producing non-identified surplus. M-step composite (maximize sum marginal item softmaxes, full multinomial joint), BIC computed usual way naive: -penalises composite likelihood. rank selection publication settings, prefer cross-validation BIC. mixLCA flags summary() output spectral_rank > 0.

title: Spectral Local Dependence: Theory and Worked Example
text: vignette("workflow") see voter_perceptions dataset SLD beats naive LCA BVR-guided direct effects, even though bivariate residuals individually large. typical case local dependence broad. smaller synthetic example makes comparison even sharper. generate N=500N = 500 observations two classes, 8 binary items. class 1, items 1-4 positively correlated via shared latent factor; class 2 items 5-8 positively correlated via different shared factor. Direct effects need enumerate (42)⋅2=12\binom{4}{2} \cdot 2 = 12 pairs absorb structure; SLD captures two rank-1 projections. SLD model captures within-class correlation one rank per class gains BIC despite extra parameters (naive BIC ≈5350\approx 5350 vs. SLD BIC ≈5188\approx 5188, savings 160 points). Inspecting loadings shows rank-1 direction class 1 bipolar contrast: items Q1-Q4 (items whose latent factor active class 1) load one sign, items Q5-Q8 load opposite sign. single dimension thereby encodes covariance pattern induced shared latent factor:

what: Relation to Latent Class Factor Analysis (LCFA)
title: Spectral Local Dependence: Theory and Worked Example
text: SLD bears surface similarity Latent Class Factor Analysis (LCFA; Magidson & Vermunt, 2001). respond problem, namely local-dependence violations LCA, two implemented different machinery. LCFA fits constrained log-linear model whose latent space Cartesian product binary factors; fit typically EM quasi-Newton inner loops factor-additive loadings. SLD keeps original KK-class latent variable applies closed-form eigendecomposition class-conditional residual Burt matrix obtain rank-dkd_k shift log response surface. LCFA implemented latent R package (lcfa()), right tool substantive theory multi-factor typological. SLD targets case user wants one KK-class typology remain intact absorb residual covariance separately.

title: A Workflow for Latent Class Analysis: Naive Fit, BVR Adjustments, and Spectral Local Dependence
text: Latent class models easy -trust. default specification assumes manifest indicators locally independent within class. , conditional class membership items carry information . assumption violated, EM algorithm compensates inventing extra classes whose purpose absorb pairwise correlation. resulting solution inflates KK, distorts class meaning, inflates standard errors downstream distal analysis. vignette walks three responses problem single dataset: naive fit treats local independence given. BVR-guided fit adds pairwise direct effects manifest items whose bivariate residuals (Vermunt, 1999) exceed chi-squared threshold. fit Spectral Local Dependence (SLD), low-rank decomposition conditional Burt matrix captures correlated structure across many items (see companion vignette vignette("sld-theory") math). use package’s embedded dataset voter_perceptions, 2000- respondent synthetic survey rating two hypothetical candidates (“” “B”) six attributes (moral character, competence, leadership, honesty, intelligence, empathy). Items come conceptually parallel pairs (moral_A/moral_B, ), priori reason expect local dependence: ratings candidate cluster together via shared partisan affect.

what: Latent Class Analysis with Local Dependence and Distal Outcomes
title: Latent Class Analysis with Local Dependence and Distal Outcomes
text: Partitioned latent class analysis R, mixed-type indicators, concomitant predictors, distal outcomes, low-rank treatment local dependence (Spectral Local Dependence, SLD). Website: https://asanaei.github.io/mixLCA/

title: Latent Class Analysis with Local Dependence and Distal Outcomes
text: Mixed indicators. Continuous (multivariate normal optional graphical-lasso penalization) categorical (product-multinomial), jointly alone. Partitioned architecture. Antecedent concomitant predictors enter via multinomial logistic regression class membership; contemporaneous manifest indicators define measurement model; subsequent distal outcomes estimated separately Bolck-Croon-Hagenaars (BCH) inverse-classification-error weighting. Local dependence. Direct effects categorical pairs (Hagenaars-Vermunt specification search via auto_bvr()) Spectral Local Dependence (auto_sld()), rank-d eigendecomposition conditional Burt matrix. Honest standard errors. Full-Hessian SEs continuous parameters, sandwich SEs NA--failure distal regressions.

title: Latent Class Analysis with Local Dependence and Distal Outcomes
text: package compiles C++ via Rcpp RcppArmadillo, need working compiler toolchain.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("asanaei/mixLCA")

code: library(mixLCA)  data("PimaIndiansDiabetes2", package = "mlbench") pima <- na.omit(PimaIndiansDiabetes2)  fit <- fit_lca(pima,                continuous  = c("glucose", "pressure", "mass", "pedigree"),                concomitant = ~ age,                n_classes   = 2,                control     = lca_control(n_starts = 10, seed = 110))  summary(fit, data = pima) plot(fit, type = "profiles")  distal(fit, pima, diabetes ~ age, family = "binomial")

title: Latent Class Analysis with Local Dependence and Distal Outcomes
text: website hosts three companion vignettes: workflow LCA: naive fit → BVR diagnostics → direct effects → SLD. Concomitant predictors distal outcomes: covariates (character vector formula), predict() semantics, BCH distal estimation. SLD theory worked example: math (conditional Burt matrix, hollow projector, identification, EM step) plus synthetic example SLD outperforms naive LCA.

what: Automated Model Selection for mixLCA — auto_bvr
title: Automated Model Selection for mixLCA — auto_bvr
text: Three-phase automated model selection: Phase 1 Select K BIC across K_range using     diagonal covariance. Phase 2a Compare diagonal, penalized, full covariance     structures continuous indicators selected K. Phase 2b Vermunt-style BVR-guided specification search     categorical indicators: iteratively add direct effects     pair largest bivariate residual, stopping BIC     longer improves max_direct_effects reached.

title: Automated Model Selection for mixLCA — auto_bvr
code: auto_bvr(   data,   continuous = NULL,   categorical = NULL,   concomitant = NULL,   K_range = 2:5,   max_direct_effects = 5L,   bvr_threshold = 3.84,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Automated Model Selection for mixLCA — auto_bvr
text: data Data frame. continuous Character vector continuous indicator names, NULL. categorical Character vector categorical indicator names, NULL. concomitant Character vector formula concomitant predictors, NULL. K_range Integer vector class counts evaluate (e.g., 2:5). max_direct_effects Integer: maximum number categorical direct effects add Phase 2b (default 5). bvr_threshold Numeric: minimum BVR chi-squared statistic consider direct effect (default 3.84, .e. p < .05 df = 1). verbose Logical: print progress? ... Additional arguments passed fit_lca (e.g., spectral_rank, spectral_pool).

title: Automated Model Selection for mixLCA — auto_bvr
text: object class mixLCA representing final selected   model, additional auto_path element recording   search trajectory.

title: Automated Model Selection for mixLCA — auto_bvr
text: Parallel execution multiple starts delegated user's active future::plan() (set session calling function).

title: Automated Model Selection for mixLCA — auto_bvr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(110) data(voter_perceptions) fit <- auto_bvr(   data        = voter_perceptions,   categorical = names(voter_perceptions),   K_range     = 3,   max_direct_effects = 4L,   n_starts    = 3,   verbose     = FALSE) fit$auto_path$direct_effects fit_indices(fit)$BIC } # }

what: Automated Class-Specific Spectral Rank Selection — auto_sld
title: Automated Class-Specific Spectral Rank Selection — auto_sld
text: Evaluates necessity Spectral Local Dependence (SLD) iteratively. Begins base model (ranks zero user-supplied fitted model) conducts forward-stepwise greedy search. step unmodeled eigenvalue spectrum inspected per class, class exhibiting largest residual eigenvalue targeted rank increment. resulting model improves information criterion, increment accepted; otherwise search terminates.

title: Automated Class-Specific Spectral Rank Selection — auto_sld
code: auto_sld(   data,   continuous = NULL,   categorical = NULL,   concomitant = NULL,   n_classes = 2L,   max_rank_per_class = 3L,   max_total_rank = 10L,   criterion = c("BIC", "aBIC", "AIC", "ICL"),   base_model = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Automated Class-Specific Spectral Rank Selection — auto_sld
text: data Data frame. continuous Character vector NULL. categorical Character vector (least 2 required). concomitant Character vector NULL. n_classes Integer: number latent classes. max_rank_per_class Integer: max spectral rank class. max_total_rank Integer: max sum ranks across classes. criterion Character: information criterion minimize ("BIC", "aBIC", "AIC", "ICL"). base_model Optional pre-fitted mixLCA object. NULL, independence model (d = 0) fitted automatically. verbose Logical: print search trajectory. ... Additional arguments forwarded fit_lca (e.g., max_iter, tol, dependence).

title: Automated Class-Specific Spectral Rank Selection — auto_sld
text: mixLCA object selected configuration,   additional element auto_spectral_path documenting   search history (data frame columns step,   class_incremented, ranks, criterion value).

title: Automated Class-Specific Spectral Rank Selection — auto_sld
text: candidate hot-started current model, latent class definitions remain anchored, preventing latent-class drift. Classes exhibit residual dependence retain d = 0, avoiding unnecessary parameter proliferation.

title: Automated Class-Specific Spectral Rank Selection — auto_sld
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(110) data(voter_perceptions) fit <- auto_sld(   data        = voter_perceptions,   categorical = names(voter_perceptions),   n_classes   = 3,   max_rank_per_class = 3L,   criterion   = "BIC",   verbose     = FALSE) fit$specs$spectral_rank      # class-specific ranks selected fit$auto_spectral_path        # accepted increments fit_indices(fit)$BIC } # }

what: Fit a Latent Class Model with mixLCA — fit_lca
title: Fit a Latent Class Model with mixLCA — fit_lca
text: Estimates finite mixture model partitioned architecture. Antecedent concomitant predictors enter via multinomial logistic regression; contemporaneous manifest indicators (continuous, categorical, mixed) define measurement model; subsequent distal outcomes estimated separately via distal BCH inverse-classification-error weighting.

title: Fit a Latent Class Model with mixLCA — fit_lca
code: fit_lca(   data,   continuous = NULL,   categorical = NULL,   concomitant = NULL,   n_classes = 2L,   dependence = "full",   penalty = "auto",   control = lca_control(),   verbose = TRUE,   cat_direct_effects = NULL,   spectral_rank = 0L,   spectral_pool = FALSE,   init_model = NULL )

title: Fit a Latent Class Model with mixLCA — fit_lca
text: data Data frame observations. continuous Character vector continuous indicator variable names, NULL. categorical Character vector categorical indicator variable names, NULL. concomitant Character vector concomitant predictor names, one-sided formula (e.g.\ ~ age * income + poly(bmi, 2)), NULL. Missing values concomitant predictors permitted; impute filter rows calling. n_classes Integer >= 2: number latent classes. dependence Character controlling covariance structure continuous indicators within class: "none" Diagonal covariance (local independence). "full" Unrestricted covariance. "penalized" Graphical-lasso penalized covariance,     guaranteeing exact sparsity positive definiteness. penalty Penalty dependence = "penalized". Either non-negative scalar string "auto" (default). "auto", heuristic value selected data; numeric, supplied value respected exactly (including 0, yields shrinkage). control List optimizer settings; see lca_control. verbose Logical: print per-start progress? cat_direct_effects List two-element character vectors specifying direct effects categorical indicators address local dependence violations. element c("parent", "child") allows child's response probabilities depend parent's observed value within class (Vermunt, 1999). Use bvr_categorical identify candidate pairs. NULL (default), standard local independence assumed categorical indicators. spectral_rank Integer (scalar length-K vector): target rank d Spectral Local Dependence (SLD) among categorical items. element > 0, SLD activated. spectral_pool Logical: Pool conditional Burt matrices across classes. init_model Optional mixLCA object warm-starting. prior model must n_classes.

title: Fit a Latent Class Model with mixLCA — fit_lca
text: object class mixLCA (list) containing: n_classes number latent classes. log_lik maximized log-likelihood final EM iterate. posteriors N x K matrix posterior class probabilities. continuous_params List class-specific means     covariances (continuous indicators used). categorical_params List class-specific category     probabilities (categorical indicators used). cat_spectral_params List class-specific Spectral Local     Dependence parameters (spectral_rank > 0). concomitant_coefs Matrix multinomial logistic regression     coefficients (concomitant predictors supplied). n_obs Number observations used estimation. convergence List detailing EM convergence status history. specs List model specifications (indicator types,     dependence structure, etc.). AIC, BIC, aBIC, ICL, entropy Cached fit indices. Treat return opaque: use provided accessors S3   methods (coef(), predict(), summary(),   get_posteriors(), get_loadings(), plot())   rather $-indexing internal fields, may   restructured future versions. Key downstream functions:   distal, fit_indices,   bvr_tests, enumerate_lca.

title: Fit a Latent Class Model with mixLCA — fit_lca
text: measurement likelihood maximized EM. Continuous indicators follow class-specific multivariate normal distributions; missing entries marginalized analytically. Categorical indicators follow product-multinomial distribution; missing categories omitted likelihood contribution. Non-diagonal covariance matrices may freely estimated (dependence = "full") estimated sparsity graphical lasso (dependence = "penalized"). Parallel execution multiple starts delegated user's active future::plan(). Set plan session (future::plan(future::multisession, workers = ...)) calling fit_lca; otherwise starts run sequentially.

title: Fit a Latent Class Model with mixLCA — fit_lca

BIC   : 19982.86  #>   Entropy           : 0.783  #>   ICL               : 20318.83  #>  #> Class Proportions: #> Class 1 Class 2  #>  0.2092  0.7908  #>  #> Continuous Indicator Means: #>         marker_1 marker_2 marker_3 marker_4 #> Class 1 130.9492  97.6235  31.1184   0.5409 #> Class 2  82.6076  73.9969  22.3791   0.3120 #>  #> Covariance (Class 1): #>           marker_1  marker_2 marker_3 marker_4 #> marker_1 3715.1944 -280.3323   9.0840   2.0554 #> marker_2 -280.3323 1789.9091 -48.7702   1.1472 #> marker_3    9.0840  -48.7702  91.9307   0.3765 #> marker_4    2.0554    1.1472   0.3765   0.0618 #>  #> Covariance (Class 2): #>          marker_1 marker_2 marker_3 marker_4 #> marker_1 727.8719  -1.4997  -1.6707   0.0540 #> marker_2  -1.4997 548.9473   1.0490  -0.0899 #> marker_3  -1.6707   1.0490  28.2157  -0.0230 #> marker_4   0.0540  -0.0899  -0.0230   0.0098 #>  #> Concomitant Coefficients (reference = Class 1): #>  #>   -> Class 2: #>              Estimate        SE      z        p     #> (Intercept)  3.490835  0.455513  7.664 1.81e-14 *** #> age         -0.047073  0.009305 -5.059 4.22e-07 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  # }

title: Latent Class Clustering — lca_clust
text: lca_clust() defines latent class model clustering via finite mixture estimation. specification designed use tidyclust framework. engine-specific details mixLCA engine documented .

title: Latent Class Clustering — lca_clust
code: lca_clust(   mode = "partition",   engine = "mixLCA",   num_clusters = NULL,   dependence = NULL,   penalty = NULL,   spectral_rank = NULL )

title: Latent Class Clustering — lca_clust
text: mode single character string type model. possible value model "partition". engine single character string specifying computational engine. Currently "mixLCA". num_clusters Positive integer, number latent classes (required). dependence Character controlling covariance structure continuous indicators: "none" (diagonal), "full" (unrestricted), "penalized" (graphical-lasso). penalty Non-negative numeric penalty graphical-lasso estimation (used dependence = "penalized"), "auto". Can set tune() grid search. spectral_rank Non-negative integer rank Spectral Local Dependence.  Zero means standard (SLD). Can set tune() grid search.

title: Latent Class Clustering — lca_clust
text: lca_clust cluster specification.

title: Latent Class Clustering — lca_clust
text: ## mean predict? predict cluster assignment new observation, model computes posterior class probabilities estimated mixture returns modal (probable) class. ## Engine arguments Additional arguments may passed underlying fit_lca() function via set_engine("mixLCA", ...). Commonly used engine arguments include: continuous Character vector continuous indicator     column names. categorical Character vector categorical indicator     column names. concomitant Character vector one-sided formula     concomitant predictors. penalty Non-negative numeric "auto". spectral_rank Integer Spectral Local     Dependence rank. control list lca_control(). neither continuous categorical specified, column types inferred automatically: numeric columns become continuous indicators factor character columns become categorical indicators.

title: Latent Class Clustering — lca_clust
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("tidyclust", quietly = TRUE)) {   data(voter_perceptions)   set.seed(110)    # --- Naive LCA (local independence) ---   spec_naive <- lca_clust(num_clusters = 3) |>     tidyclust::set_engine("mixLCA",       categorical = names(voter_perceptions),       control = lca_control(n_starts = 3))    fit_naive <- tidyclust::fit(spec_naive, ~ ., data = voter_perceptions)   predict(fit_naive, new_data = voter_perceptions)    # --- SLD clustering (spectral local dependence, rank 2) ---   spec_sld <- lca_clust(num_clusters = 3, spectral_rank = 2L) |>     tidyclust::set_engine("mixLCA",       categorical = names(voter_perceptions),       control = lca_control(n_starts = 3))    fit_sld <- tidyclust::fit(spec_sld, ~ ., data = voter_perceptions)   predict(fit_sld, new_data = voter_perceptions)    # Compare fit indices   cat("Naive BIC:", BIC(fit_naive$fit),       " SLD BIC:", BIC(fit_sld$fit), "\n") } #> Note: `dependence` and `penalty` only affect continuous indicators. With categorical-only models they have no effect. #> Note: `dependence` and `penalty` only affect continuous indicators. With categorical-only models they have no effect. #> Naive BIC: 61256.36  SLD BIC: 61791.25  # }

text: simulated dataset 2000 respondents rating two hypothetical political candidates ("" "B") six attributes: moral character (moral), competence (compet), leadership (lead), honesty (honest), intelligence (intel), empathy (empath). attribute appears twice, candidate, distinguished suffix _A _B.

text: data frame 2000 rows 12 columns. columns   ordered factors levels poor < fair < good < excellent. moral_A, moral_B Rating moral character candidates B. compet_A, compet_B Rating competence. lead_A, lead_B Rating leadership. honest_A, honest_B Rating honesty. intel_A, intel_B Rating intelligence. empath_A, empath_B Rating empathy.

title: Synthetic Voter Perceptions of Two Hypothetical Candidates — voter_perceptions
text: Synthetic data, generated   data-raw/make-example-data.R.

title: Synthetic Voter Perceptions of Two Hypothetical Candidates — voter_perceptions
text: Ratings ordered four-level factors (poor, fair, good, excellent). generative model three-class mixture: strong -favoring class (40%), strong B-favoring class (40%), moderate / ambivalent class (20%). Within class, shared latent factor induces local dependence six items rating candidate; makes dataset useful demonstrating bivariate residual diagnostics (bvr_categorical) spectral local dependence (auto_sld). data synthetic, authored package author didactic purposes, licensed GPL (>= 3) along rest package. reproducible generation script provided source tree data-raw/make-example-data.R. substantive analog (source) rating-scale design used American National Election Studies (ANES), respondents rate presidential candidates attribute dimensions. published analyses real ANES data, see example Bartle (2000) related political-cognition literature.

title: Synthetic Voter Perceptions of Two Hypothetical Candidates — voter_perceptions
text: Bartle, J. (2000). Political awareness, opinion   constraint stability ideological positions.   Political Studies, 48(3), 467-484.
